{{Short description|American entrepreneur (born 1983)}}
{{Use mdy dates|date=February 2026}}
{{Infobox scientist
| name              = Dario Amodei
| | image             = Dario Amodei at TechCrunch Disrupt 2023 01 (cropped).jpg
| | caption           = Amodei in 2023
| | birth_date        = {{Birth year and age|1983}}<ref name=":3" />
| | birth_place       = [[San Francisco]], California, U.S.
| | death_date        = 
| | death_place       = 
| | fields            = [[Artificial intelligence]]
| | workplaces        = {{ubl |[[Stanford University School of Medicine]] |[[OpenAI]] |Anthropic}}
| | education         = {{ubl |[[California Institute of Technology]] (attended) |[[Stanford University]] ([[Bachelor of Science|BS]]) |[[Princeton University]] ([[Master of Science|MS]], [[Doctor of Philosophy|PhD]])}}
| | thesis_title      = Network-Scale Electrophysiology: Measuring and Understanding the Collective Behavior of Neural Circuits
| | thesis_url        = https://dataspace.princeton.edu/handle/88435/dsp013f462544k
| | thesis_year       = 2011
| | doctoral_advisor  = Michael J. Berry<br/>[[William Bialek]]
| | known_for         = Co-founder and CEO of [[Anthropic]]
| | signature         = 
| | relatives         = [[Daniela Amodei]] (sister)
| | awards            = [[Time 100|''Time'' 100]] (2025)
| | website           = {{url|https://darioamodei.com}}
|}}

'''Dario Amodei''' (born 1983) is an American [[artificial intelligence]] (AI) researcher and entrepreneur. In 2021, he and his sister [[Daniela Amodei]] co-founded [[Anthropic]], the company behind the [[large language model]] series [[Claude (language model)|Claude]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Perrigo |first=Billy |date=30 May 2024 |title=Inside Anthropic, the AI Company Betting That Safety Can Be a Winning Strategy |url=https://time.com/6980000/anthropic/ |access-date=3 October 2025 |website=Time}}</ref> Prior to that, he was the vice president of research at [[OpenAI]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Roose |first=Kevin |date=July 11, 2023 |title=Inside the White-Hot Center of A.I. Doomerism |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/07/11/technology/anthropic-ai-claude-chatbot.html |newspaper=New York Times |url-access=registration}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Oreskovic |first=Alexei |date=July 11, 2023 |title=Anthropic CEO A.I. risks: short, medium, and long-term |url=https://fortune.com/2023/07/10/anthropic-ceo-dario-amodei-ai-risks-short-medium-long-term/ |magazine=Fortune}}</ref>

In his capacity as Anthropic's [[Chief executive officer|CEO]], Amodei often writes on the benefits and risks of advanced AI systems.<ref name=":4">{{Cite news |last=Kessler |first=Sarah |date=7 December 2025 |title=Dario Amodei on the Risk of an A.I. Bubble, Regulation and A.G.I. |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2025/12/07/business/dealbook/dario-amodei-dealbook.html |access-date=9 December 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}}</ref> He is a proponent of an "entente" strategy in which a coalition of democratic nations use advanced AI systems in military applications to achieve a decisive advantage over adversaries while sharing the benefits with cooperating nations.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2">{{Cite web |last=Murgia |first=Madhumita |date=2024-12-05 |title=Anthropic’s Dario Amodei: Democracies must maintain the lead in AI |url=https://www.ft.com/content/e75e3388-4700-413d-ab67-778410c2d977 |access-date=2025-08-12 |website=Financial Times}}</ref>

== Early life and education ==
Dario Amodei was born in [[San Francisco]], California, in 1983.<ref name=":3" /> His sister, [[Daniela Amodei|Daniela]], was born four years later.<ref name=":3" /> His father, Riccardo Amodei, an Italian-American leather craftsman from [[Massa Marittima]], [[Tuscany]], died when Amodei was a young adult.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Levy |first=Steven |date=2025-03-28 |title=If Anthropic Succeeds, a Nation of Benevolent AI Geniuses Could Be Born |url=https://www.wired.com/story/anthropic-benevolent-artificial-intelligence/ |access-date=2026-02-21 |work=Wired |language=en-US |issn=1059-1028}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Frasso |first=Edoardo |date=2023-11-27 |title=Chi sono Dario e Daniela Amodei: gli italoamericani dell'AI etica |url=https://ainews.it/chi-sono-dario-e-daniela-amodei-gli-italoamericani-delletica-tech/ |access-date=2026-02-27 |website=AI News |language=it-IT}}</ref> His mother, Elena Engel, a Jewish American born in [[Chicago]], worked as a [[project manager]] for libraries.<ref name=":3">{{Cite news |last=Levy |first=Steven |title=If Anthropic Succeeds, a Nation of Benevolent AI Geniuses Could Be Born |url=https://www.wired.com/story/anthropic-benevolent-artificial-intelligence/ |access-date=2025-03-28 |work=Wired |language=en-US |issn=1059-1028}}</ref>

Dario grew up in San Francisco and graduated from [[Lowell High School (San Francisco)|Lowell High School]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=August 23, 2014 |title=Lowell Alumni Newsletter Winter 2008 by Lowell Alumni Association |url=https://issuu.com/lowell_alumni_association/docs/laa_winter08 |website=Issuu |language=en |access-date=July 17, 2024 |archive-date=February 21, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250221215644/https://issuu.com/lowell_alumni_association/docs/laa_winter08 |url-status=dead }}</ref> He was a member of the [[United States Physics Olympiad|USA Physics Olympiad team]] in 2000.<ref name="Physics Olympics-2000">{{cite web | url= https://www.aapt.org/olympiad2000/team2000.html | title=2000 US Physics olympiad team }}</ref> Amodei began college at [[Caltech]], where he worked with [[Tom Tombrello]] as one of his Physics 11 students. He later transferred to [[Stanford University]], where he received a bachelor of science degree in physics.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Fuller-Wright |first=Liz |date=September 12, 2023 |title=TIME Magazine's TIME100 artificial intelligence list honors six Princetonians |url=https://www.princeton.edu/news/2023/09/12/time-magazines-time100-artificial-intelligence-list-honors-six-princetonians |publisher=Princeton University |language=en}}</ref> He also holds a PhD in biophysics from [[Princeton University]], where he studied [[electrophysiology]] of [[neural circuit]]s.<ref>{{Cite thesis |last=Amodei |first=Dario |date=2011 |title=Network-Scale Electrophysiology: Measuring and Understanding the Collective Behavior of Neural Circuits |url=https://dataspace.princeton.edu/handle/88435/dsp013f462544k |language=en}}</ref> He was a postdoctoral scholar at the [[Stanford University School of Medicine]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dario Amodei, PhD |url=https://www.hertzfoundation.org/person/dario-amodei/ |publisher=The Hertz Foundation}}</ref>

== Career ==
From November 2014 until October 2015, he worked at [[Baidu]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Griffiths |first=Brent D. |title=Meet Dario Amodei, Anthropic's outspoken CEO |url=https://www.businessinsider.com/dario-amodei |access-date=2025-10-03 |website=Business Insider |language=en-US}}</ref> After that, he worked at [[Google]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dario Amodei |url=https://www.linkedin.com/in/dario-amodei-3934934/ |website=LinkedIn}}</ref> In 2016, Amodei joined [[OpenAI]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hao |first=Karen |date=February 17, 2020 |title=The messy, secretive reality behind OpenAI's bid to save the world |url=https://www.technologyreview.com/2020/02/17/844721/ai-openai-moonshot-elon-musk-sam-altman-greg-brockman-messy-secretive-reality/ |publisher=MIT Technology Review}}</ref>

In 2021, Dario and his sister, Daniela, founded [[Anthropic]] along with other former senior members of OpenAI.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2023-09-07 |title=TIME100 AI 2023: Dario and Daniela Amodei |url=https://time.com/collection/time100-ai/6309047/daniela-and-dario-amodei/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250615221425/https://time.com/collection/time100-ai/6309047/daniela-and-dario-amodei/ |archive-date=2025-06-15 |access-date=2025-10-03 |work=Time |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Varanasi |first=Lakshmi |date=2025-12-04 |title=Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei drags OpenAI and Google: 'We don't have to do any code reds' |url=https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-ceo-dario-amodei-drags-openai-and-google-code-red-2025-12 |access-date=2025-12-09 |website=Business Insider |language=en-US}}</ref> The Amodei siblings were among those who left OpenAI due to directional differences.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Goldman |first=Sharon |date=April 7, 2023 |title=As Anthropic seeks billions to take on OpenAI, 'industrial capture' is nigh. Or is it? |url=https://venturebeat.com/ai/as-anthropic-seeks-billions-to-take-on-openai-industrial-capture-is-nigh-or-is-it/ |website=VentureBeat |language=en-US}}</ref> 

In November 2023, the board of directors of OpenAI approached Amodei about replacing [[Sam Altman]] and potentially merging the two startups. Amodei declined both offers.<ref>{{Cite web |last1=Dastin |first1=Jeffrey |date=November 21, 2023 |title=OpenAI's board approached Anthropic CEO about top job and merger |url=https://www.reuters.com/technology/openais-board-approached-anthropic-ceo-about-top-job-merger-sources-2023-11-21/ |publisher=Reuters}}</ref>

In 2025, ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' magazine listed Amodei as one of [[Time 100|the world's 100 most influential people]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Kutcher |first1=Ashton |author1-link=Ashton Kutcher |title=Dario Amodei: The 100 Most Influential People of 2025 |url=https://time.com/collections/100-most-influential-people-2025/7273747/dario-amodei |website=TIME |access-date=18 April 2025 |language=en |date=16 April 2025}}</ref> He also was named as one of the "Architects of AI" for ''Time''{{'s}} [[Time Person of the Year|Person of the Year]].<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cly01mdm577o |title='Architects of AI' named Time Magazine's Person of the Year |date=December 10, 2025 |work=BBC News |last1=Rahman-Jones |first1=Imran |last2=McMahon |first2=Liv}}</ref>

{{As of|2026|02|post=,}} Anthropic has an estimated value of $380 billion,<ref>{{Cite web |title=Anthropic raises $30 billion in Series G funding at $380 billion post-money valuation |url=https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-raises-30-billion-series-g-funding-380-billion-post-money-valuation |access-date=2026-02-12 |website=www.anthropic.com |language=en}}</ref> with ''[[Forbes]]'' estimating Amodei's net worth to be $7 billion.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Nieva |first=Richard |title=Despite Digs From Musk And Altman, Anthropic's Billionaire Founders Double Their Fortunes |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/richardnieva/2026/02/12/anthropics-seven-billionaire-cofounders-nearly-double-their-fortunes-as-ai-giants-valuation-soars-to-380-billion/ |archive-url=http://web.archive.org/web/20260219051549/https://www.forbes.com/sites/richardnieva/2026/02/12/anthropics-seven-billionaire-cofounders-nearly-double-their-fortunes-as-ai-giants-valuation-soars-to-380-billion/ |archive-date=2026-02-19 |access-date=2026-02-20 |work=Forbes |language=en}}</ref>

== Views ==
=== Benefits of AI ===
In October 2024, Amodei published an essay entitled "Machines of Loving Grace", in which he speculated about how AI could improve human welfare.<ref name=":1">{{Cite news |last=Sullivan |first=Mark |date=October 17, 2024 |title=Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei pens a smart look at our AI future |url=https://www.fastcompany.com/91211163/anthropic-ceo-dario-amodei-pens-a-smart-look-at-our-ai-future |work=Fast Company}}</ref> In it, he writes, "I think that most people are underestimating just how radical the upside of AI could be, just as I think most people are underestimating how bad the risks could be."<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |last=Amodei |first=Dario |date=2024-10-11 |title=Dario Amodei — Machines of Loving Grace |url=https://darioamodei.com/machines-of-loving-grace |access-date=2024-11-18 |website= |language=en}}</ref> The essay described a vision of civilization where the risks of AI had been addressed and powerful AI was applied to raise the quality of life for everyone, suggesting that AI could contribute to enormous advances in biology, neuroscience, economic development, global peace, work, and meaning of lives.<ref name=":0"/>

In the article, Amodei also stresses the importance "that democracies have the upper hand on the world stage when powerful AI is created", and argues for an "entente" strategy where a coalition of democracies use AI to achieve a decisive strategic and military advantage over their adversaries, while distributing the benefits to all cooperating democratic nations.<ref name=":0" />

=== Risks of AI ===
In January 2026, Amodei published a follow-up essay entitled "The Adolescence of Technology", which focuses on the risks posed by powerful AI<ref name=":5">{{Cite web |last=Amodei |first=Dario |title=Dario Amodei — The Adolescence of Technology |url=https://darioamodei.com/essay/the-adolescence-of-technology |date=January 2026|access-date= |website= |language=en}}</ref><ref name="hammond">{{cite news| last1 = Hammond| first1 = George| last2 = Heikkilä| first2 = Melissa| title ='Humanity needs to wake up' to dangers of AI, says Anthropic chief| url = https://www.ft.com/content/c3098552-7204-4a93-844c-1b8569c9dcb2 | work = Financial Times| date = 2026-01-26}}</ref><ref name="milmo">{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jan/27/wake-up-to-the-risks-of-ai-they-are-almost-here-anthropic-boss-warns |title=Wake up to the risks of AI, they are almost here, Anthropic boss warns | last=Milmo | first=Dan |work=The Guardian |date=2026-01-27 |access-date=2026-01-29}}</ref> and expands on his earlier statements about these risks.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Morrone |first=Megan |date=2025-09-17 |title=Amodei on AI: "There's a 25% chance that things go really, really badly" |url=https://www.axios.com/2025/09/17/anthropic-dario-amodei-p-doom-25-percent |access-date=2025-10-21 |website=Axios |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Ramkumar |first=Berber Jin and Amrith |date=2025-09-19 |title=A Tech CEO's Lonely Fight Against Trump |url=https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/ai-anthropic-dario-amodei-david-sacks-9c1a771c |access-date=2025-10-21 |website=The Wall Street Journal |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=July 25, 2023 |title=Anthropic's Amodei Warns US Senators of AI-Powered Weapons |url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2023-07-25/anthropic-s-amodei-warns-us-senators-of-ai-powered-bioweapons |url-access=subscription |publisher=Bloomberg}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |date=2023-07-25 |title=AI leaders warn Congress that AI could be used to create bioweapons |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/technology/2023/07/25/ai-bengio-anthropic-senate-hearing/ |access-date=2025-10-03 |work=The Washington Post |language=en-US |issn=0190-8286}}</ref> In the essay, Amodei identifies five major categories of AI risk.

The first category concerns the possibility that AI systems develop goals or behaviors [[AI alignment|misaligned with human intentions]]. He notes that such behaviors have already been observed in testing at Anthropic, including AI models engaging in deception, blackmail, and scheming.<ref name=":5" /><ref name="hammond"/>

The second category involves misuse of AI for destruction by individuals or small groups, with Amodei expressing particular concern about [[biological weapon]]s. He warns that AI could enable people without specialized training to create [[weapon of mass destruction|weapons of mass destruction]].<ref name=":5" /><ref name="hammond"/>

The third category concerns misuse of AI by powerful actors to seize or maintain power. Amodei cautions that AI could enable authoritarian governments to conduct unprecedented [[surveillance]], deploy [[autonomous weapon]]s, and engage in mass [[propaganda]]. He identifies the [[Chinese Communist Party]] as the greatest threat in this regard, arguing that democracies must maintain AI leadership to prevent a "global totalitarian dictatorship".<ref>{{Cite news |last=Manson |first=Katrina |date=February 25, 2026 |title=Anthropic’s Pentagon Showdown Is About More Than AI Guardrails |url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2026-02-26/pentagon-pressures-anthropic-to-drop-ai-guardrails-in-military-standoff?srnd=homepage-americas |access-date=February 25, 2026 |work=Bloomberg}}</ref>

The fourth category addresses economic disruption, including [[technological unemployment|mass labor displacement]] and concentration of wealth. Amodei notes that AI could displace half of all entry-level white-collar jobs within one to five years, and warns of wealth concentration exceeding that of the [[Gilded Age]], with personal fortunes potentially reaching into the trillions of dollars.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Vlamis |first=Kelsey |date=2025-09-18 |title=Anthropic cofounders say the likelihood of AI replacing human jobs is so high that they needed to warn the world about it |url=https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-ceo-warning-world-ai-replacing-jobs-necessary-2025-9 |access-date=2025-10-21 |website=Business Insider |language=en-US}}</ref>

The fifth category encompasses indirect effects and unknown factors, including rapid advances in biology that could alter human lifespans or human intelligence, unhealthy changes to human life from AI interaction, and challenges to human purpose in a world where AI exceeds human capabilities across virtually all domains.<ref name=":5" />

=== Dispute with the Department of Defense ===
{{Main|Anthropic–United States Department of Defense dispute}}
In February 2026, Amodei refused to remove a contractual ban on [[Claude (language model)|Claude]] usage for mass domestic surveillance or fully autonomous weapons, as requested by [[Pete Hegseth]]'s [[United States Department of Defense|Department of Defense]] (DoD). Anthropic was subsequently labeled a DoD "supply-chain risk" and the Trump administration ordered US agencies to stop using Claude. The Pentagon denied interest in such use cases but insisted on unrestricted access.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2026-02-27 |title=Trump orders US agencies to stop using Anthropic technology in clash over AI safety |url=https://apnews.com/article/anthropic-pentagon-ai-hegseth-dario-amodei-b72d1894bc842d9acf026df3867bee8a |access-date=2026-03-11 |website=AP News |language=en}}</ref> The DoD's actions have been widely condemned as illegal retaliation against [[Freedom of speech|protected speech]] with several organizations filing [[amicus briefs]] supporting Anthropic. On March 26, 2026, a federal judge issued a [[Injunction|temporary injunction]] against the DoD, agreeing their actions appeared to be "classic [[First Amendment to the United States Constitution|First Amendment]] retaliation."<ref name=":02">{{Cite web |last=Tuccille |first=J. D. |date=2026-03-11 |title=The federal government’s crusade against Anthropic raises First Amendment concerns |url=https://reason.com/2026/03/11/the-federal-governments-crusade-against-anthropic-raises-first-amendment-concerns/ |access-date=2026-03-31 |website=Reason.com |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name=":12">{{Cite web |date=March 10, 2026 |title=The Pentagon’s Retaliation Campaign Against Anthropic Is Unconstitutional |url=https://www.cato.org/blog/pentagons-retaliation-campaign-against-anthropic-unconstitutional |access-date=2026-03-31 |website=www.cato.org}}</ref><ref name=":22">{{Cite web |last=Brown |first=Elizabeth Nolan |date=2026-03-30 |title=Government actions against Anthropic are ‘classic First Amendment retaliation’ |url=https://reason.com/2026/03/30/government-actions-against-anthropic-are-classic-first-amendment-retaliation/ |access-date=2026-03-31 |website=Reason.com |language=en-US}}</ref>

== References ==
{{reflist}}

== External links ==
{{Commonscat}}
*{{cite web|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/07/21/podcasts/dario-amodei-ceo-of-anthropic-on-the-paradoxes-of-ai-safety-and-netflixs-deep-fake-love.html |title=Dario Amodei, C.E.O. of Anthropic, on the Paradoxes of A.I. Safety and Netflix's 'Deep Fake Love' |newspaper=New York Times |date=July 21, 2023 |url-access=registration |format=podcast}}
*
*{{Time Persons of the Year 2001–2025}}
*
*{{Authority control}}
*
*{{DEFAULTSORT:Amodei, Dario}}
*[[Category:1983 births]]
*[[Category:Living people]]
*[[Category:American billionaires]]
*[[Category:American people of Italian descent]]
*[[Category:American people of Jewish descent]]
*[[Category:Anthropic people]]
*[[Category:OpenAI people]]
*[[Category:Stanford University alumni]]
*[[Category:Princeton University alumni]]
*[[Category:Lowell High School (San Francisco) alumni]]
*[[Category:People from San Francisco]]
*[[Category:Time Person of the Year]]
*
